hard.next_step: 10% confidence 26th May- Project is on freeze due to geo politics issues- no compelling need at present- follow up end June 12th Feb- initial interest from Group CIO (known to Zycus). Appears as potential replacement of Coupa P2P Will be in Dubai 25th to meet F2F
hard.ais_why: NextStep is a single thin 12 Feb entry framing the deal as a Coupa P2P replacement with no AI mention; Merlin and Merlin Intake are bundled by Zycus but absent from the buyer conversation, so AI is a Zycus value-add not a buyer demand.
hard.competitor: Do Nothing / Freeze; Coupa; SAP Ariba

north_star_verdict.headline: Early-stage P2P evaluation frozen since 26 May 2026: the buyer put the project on hold citing geopolitics and no compelling need, and has gone silent for 108 days after a single 19 Mar overview call.
north_star_verdict.math: Created and Qualified 12 Feb 2026 (144 days ago); still in Qualified. Last verified buyer activity 19 Mar 2026 (108 days ago, the only recorded Avoma call). Next_Step__c 26 May 2026 states the project is frozen for geopolitics with no compelling need and a promised end-June follow-up that has not occurred as of 6 Jul 2026. Close date 12 Oct 2026 is 98 days out. A Qualified deal dark for 108 days with an explicit buyer-stated freeze and a lapsed follow-up is Off Track.
evidence: Next_Step__c 26 May 2026: 'Project is on freeze due to geo politics issues- no compelling need at present- follow up end June'
LastActivityDate 19 Mar 2026 (108 days ago), matching the only recorded Avoma call
No Task, Event, or Avoma activity since 19 Mar 2026; promised end-June follow-up has not occurred as of 6 Jul 2026
Subodh Kumar Dubey on the 19 Mar 2026 call: 'this is a preliminary call... we don't know what solution is the right fit' and proposed an April deep-dive workshop that was never scheduled
Stage Qualified since 12 Feb 2026; forecast Pipeline with a 10% confidence annotation in Next_Step
Owner reassigned 29 Jun 2026 (Carl Kimball to Mohamad Alhakim) with no accompanying buyer re-engagement

metrics.narrative: Real operational pain is articulated but never quantified. On the 19 Mar 2026 call Gautam Anand (Group Head IT) said the group is 'primarily looking at p two p', already runs OpenText for invoicing 'which I would say is not implemented very effectively', has no vendor portal 'where vendors can come in and upload the invoicing', and wants '3 way match and all those things' integrated with SAP ECC. Subodh Kumar Dubey (Group CIO) added the strategic driver: a move from distributed to 'a centralized model' and 'a complete shared services model for procurement'. Carl Kimball (Zycus) offered a value claim ('60 to 70% reduction in TCO' via the iSaaS integration) but this is a Zycus figure, not a buyer-owned number. No savings target, cycle-time goal, AP headcount, or error-rate baseline was stated by the buyer, and the 26 May freeze note ('no compelling need at present') confirms quantified urgency was never established. The move on re-engagement is to co-build a value case (OpenText licence elimination, vendor-portal cycle time, three-way-match error reduction) with the supply-chain and finance leads.
sources: Avoma call 19 Mar 2026, Gautam Anand: 'looking at a p two p solution... get rid of Opentext... vendor portal... basic controls like 3 way match... integrated with SAP'
Avoma call 19 Mar 2026, Subodh Kumar Dubey: 'we intend to get into a centralized model... a complete shared services model for procurement'
Next_Step__c 26 May 2026: 'no compelling need at present'

economic_buyer.narrative: Subodh Kumar Dubey (Group Chief Information Officer, the PRIMARY Decision Maker contact role) is the senior figure driving the evaluation and framed the commercial question himself on 19 Mar 2026: 'why we should go for a complete procure to pay solution... what is the investment'. That signals he owns or gates the IT budget for this decision, but the true funding owner for a group-wide P2P and shared-services program (Group CFO or business-unit leadership) was never named or on a call. Gautam Anand (Group Head IT, Executive Sponsor role) is operational, not commercial. No pricing, proposal, or budget conversation has taken place, and the buyer explicitly deferred: 'why we should go... what is the investment' remained an open question for Zycus to answer. Because access is single-threaded to two IT leaders with no finance or procurement-leadership contact, the economic buyer is mapped in title only; confirming who signs the investment and engaging them is a ranked move.
sources: SF OpportunityContactRole: Subodh Kumar Dubey, Group CIO, Decision Maker, PRIMARY
Avoma call 19 Mar 2026, Subodh Kumar Dubey: 'why we should go for a complete procure to pay solution... what is the investment'
SF OpportunityContactRole: only two buyer roles, both IT; no finance or procurement-leadership contact

decision_criteria.narrative: The buyer stated its evaluation criteria clearly on 19 Mar 2026, shaped by two prior failures. Configurability and flexibility for a diverse conglomerate is the dominant criterion: Subodh Kumar Dubey pushed back hard on standard-fit claims, 'reaching even 80% of this kind of standard configuration product is easier said than done... each business has different dynamics... that's the challenge we faced with Coupa and Ariba', and asked directly 'how flexible and customized the solution is'. Speed of implementation is second: Gautam Anand wanted 'a solution which can be implemented very quickly', against Zycus's stated 5-to-7-month P2P timeline. Native SAP integration (ECC today, S/4HANA in the H2-2026 transformation) is a hard requirement, along with a vendor portal, three-way match, and OpenText replacement. Criteria are confirmed and buyer-stated, though the formal requirements set is not yet documented with Zycus.
sources: Avoma call 19 Mar 2026, Subodh Kumar Dubey: 'how flexible and customized the solution is... that's the challenge we faced with Coupa and Ariba'
Avoma call 19 Mar 2026, Gautam Anand: 'a solution which can be implemented very quickly... integrated with SAP, we are an SAP ECC as of today'
Avoma call 19 Mar 2026, Subodh Kumar Dubey: ERP transformation 'second half of this year... 12 to 18 month exercise' to S/4HANA

decision_process.narrative: The 19 Mar 2026 call was an explicitly preliminary, IT-only session (Subodh Kumar Dubey and Gautam Anand on the buyer side; Carl Kimball and Tanya for Zycus). The buyer defined the intended next step: 'we need to get into a workshop of two or two and a half hour... along with my business team and the complex business environment people' with 'supply chain team also get involved', and named Gautam Anand as the point of contact who 'will internally discuss... and give you one or two slots... probably in April'. The evaluation was scoped to run into H2 2026 alongside the ERP transformation. That process then stalled: the April workshop was never scheduled and the 26 May freeze halted the cycle. The approval chain (who signs off on the investment) is unmapped. The process the buyer described is real but has not advanced past the intro stage.
sources: Avoma call 19 Mar 2026, Subodh Kumar Dubey: 'we need to get into a workshop... along with my business team... supply chain team also get involved'
Avoma call 19 Mar 2026, Subodh Kumar Dubey: 'Gautam will be the point of contact... propose one or two slots... probably in April'
Next_Step__c 26 May 2026: project frozen; the proposed April workshop never occurred

paper_process.narrative: No contracting mechanics have surfaced, which is expected for a Qualified-stage deal that has had a single overview call and is now frozen. There is no discussion of MSA, Order Form, SOW, procurement channel, legal review, or InfoSec, and no SI or implementation partner has been named. The only forward-looking paper signal is contextual: the group is entering a 12-to-18-month S/4HANA ERP transformation in H2 2026, and the incumbent tools being displaced are OpenText (invoicing) and, for sourcing only, Coupa. Visibility into the paper process would come only after the buyer restarts the evaluation and reaches a fit workshop and commercials; it is premature and not a current risk at this stage.
sources: Avoma call 19 Mar 2026: no MSA/SOW/legal/InfoSec/procurement discussion across the call
Avoma call 19 Mar 2026, Subodh Kumar Dubey: S/4HANA ERP transformation planned H2 2026 over 12-18 months

identify_pain.narrative: The operational pain is clearly confirmed and owned by IT. Gautam Anand (Group Head IT) named the concrete pain on 19 Mar 2026: OpenText for invoice processing is 'not implemented very effectively in use', there is no 'vendor portal where vendors can come in and upload the invoicing', and the group lacks integrated three-way-match controls. Subodh Kumar Dubey (Group CIO) articulated the structural pain: procurement is 'distributed' across a diverse conglomerate (projects, retail, distribution, manufacturing, real estate) with only minimal shared services, and the group wants to centralize but 'it is a big change for the organization'. Both prior tools disappointed on fit ('similar case we had with Ariba... Coupa might not be the right fit'). The pain is real and articulated, but the pain owner sits in IT; the business and supply-chain users who feel the day-to-day pain have not yet been brought into the conversation.
sources: Avoma call 19 Mar 2026, Gautam Anand: OpenText 'not implemented very effectively... no vendor portal... 3 way match'
Avoma call 19 Mar 2026, Subodh Kumar Dubey: 'currently it is distributed, but we intend to get into a centralized model... it is a big change'

champion.narrative: No champion exists. The relationship is single-threaded to two IT leaders, Subodh Kumar Dubey (Group CIO) and Gautam Anand (Group Head IT), who are the only buyer participants on the only recorded call. Their posture is evaluative and openly skeptical, not advocacy: Subodh led with 'this is a preliminary call... we don't know what solution is the right fit' and cited the Coupa and Ariba fit failures as a reason to be cautious. Neither has driven the deal internally, introduced Zycus to procurement, finance, or business-unit leadership, shared requirements, or pushed back on alternatives on Zycus's behalf. The proof of the absence is behavioural: the April workshop Subodh himself proposed was never scheduled, the deal froze on 26 May, and no buyer-initiated contact has occurred in 108 days. Developing a champion at the operational pain-owner level (supply chain or a business-unit procurement lead) is a prerequisite to any restart.
sources: SF OpportunityContactRole: only two buyer roles, both IT (Subodh Kumar Dubey, Gautam Anand); none in procurement, finance, or a business unit
Avoma call 19 Mar 2026, Subodh Kumar Dubey: 'this is a preliminary call... we don't know what solution is the right fit'
No buyer-initiated activity since 19 Mar 2026; proposed April workshop never scheduled; Next_Step__c 26 May 2026 freeze

competition.narrative: The strongest current threat is inertia, not a rival vendor. As of 26 May 2026 the project is frozen with 'no compelling need at present' and no restart date, so Do Nothing is the live competitor. Neither named rival is in active P2P consideration: SAP Ariba was used for sourcing and vendor onboarding 'for many years till last year' and has been replaced (declined), and Coupa was implemented 'a couple of months back' for sourcing and vendor onboarding only, where the buyer already 'realized this might not be the right fit for us' (faded, and not a P2P contender). No live vendor bake-off is running; Zycus was the only P2P vendor engaged as of 19 Mar 2026. The real obstacles are buyer inertia plus SaaS-fit skepticism earned from the Coupa and Ariba experience. The wedge is to convert those two fit failures into proof of Zycus configurability (app-extend, iSaaS SAP integration) in a live requirements-mapping session before advancing.
sources: Next_Step__c 26 May 2026: 'Project is on freeze... no compelling need at present'
Avoma call 19 Mar 2026, Subodh Kumar Dubey: 'we used SAP Ariba for many years till last year... the same module we replaced with Coupa... we realized this might not be the right fit for us'

deal_movement.summary: The deal peaked at a single 19 Mar 2026 IT overview call and has regressed since: the buyer-proposed April workshop never happened, the project was frozen on 26 May 2026 for geopolitics with no compelling need, and the promised end-June follow-up has lapsed. The only recent CRM change is an internal owner reassignment on 29 Jun 2026 to Mohamad Alhakim, not a buyer signal.

competitive_position.summary: The single strongest current threat is Do Nothing / project freeze (active since 26 May 2026 with no restart date or compelling event). SAP Ariba was replaced in 2025 and is out of P2P consideration (declined); Coupa was implemented in early 2026 for sourcing and vendor onboarding only and already judged a poor fit by the buyer on 19 Mar 2026, so it is not a live P2P contender (faded). No active vendor bake-off exists; Zycus was the only P2P vendor engaged. The win path is defeating inertia and SaaS-fit skepticism, not out-competing a rival.

competitors.name: Do Nothing / Freeze
competitors.quote: Project is on freeze due to geo politics issues- no compelling need at present- follow up end June
competitors.how_we_win: Attach a compelling event: tie P2P to the H2-2026 S/4HANA transformation and OpenText replacement, and offer a fast-start vendor-portal quick win so the buyer can move without a full commitment during the geopolitical uncertainty.

competitors.name: Coupa
competitors.quote: we implemented a couple of months back... but when we got into the onboarding, we realized this might not be the right fit for us
competitors.source: Avoma call 19 Mar 2026, Subodh Kumar Dubey
competitors.how_we_win: Coupa is live only for sourcing and vendor onboarding, not P2P, and is already failing on fit. Use it as proof that Zycus app-extend configurability plus iSaaS SAP integration close the flexibility gap Coupa left open; prove 80%+ fit in a live requirements session.

competitors.name: SAP Ariba
competitors.quote: we used SAP Ariba for many years till last year... similar case we had with Ariba
competitors.source: Avoma call 19 Mar 2026, Subodh Kumar Dubey
competitors.how_we_win: Ariba was replaced in 2025 and is not in P2P consideration. The buyer's stated dissatisfaction with Ariba's fit for a complex conglomerate is a wedge for Zycus configurability, but only if fit is proven explicitly rather than assumed.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Fit expectations are dominated by configurability skepticism earned from Coupa and Ariba. Zycus aligns well on SAP integration but is exposed on the buyer's core doubt that any SaaS product can hit their real-world fit, and on the tension between the buyer's 'quick' implementation wish and Zycus's 5-to-7-month timeline.

best_practice_check.summary: Three levers decide whether this restarts: break out of the IT-only thread, convert the Coupa/Ariba fit failures into a proof-of-fit wedge, and manufacture a compelling event to defeat the freeze.
flags: Break the single-thread: the deal is confined to two IT leaders (Subodh, Gautam); reach a supply-chain or business-unit procurement lead and a finance budget owner to seed a real champion and map the economic buyer.
Turn the Coupa and Ariba fit failures into the wedge: run a live requirements-mapping session proving 80%+ standard fit plus app-extend and iSaaS SAP integration before asking to advance.
Create a compelling event to beat the freeze: anchor P2P to the H2-2026 S/4HANA transformation and OpenText replacement so there is a dated forcing function the buyer owns.

Neither has driven the deal internally, introduced Zycus beyond IT, or acted to overcome the freeze; the 108-day silence and the unscheduled April workshop confirm the absence.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Zycus led heavily with Merlin and Merlin Intake (Teams-embedded intake, agentic AI examples, iSaaS) on 19 Mar 2026, but the buyer gave no reaction and raised no AI requirement for procurement; the AI story is being pushed by Zycus, not pulled by the buyer, so it is under-positioned as a value driver here.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS field reads 3 / Negative because the thin NextStep frames the deal as a Coupa P2P replacement with no AI mention. The 19 Mar call adds nuance: the group is running its own agentic-AI pilots for sales and customer service ('we already started on some of the use cases... we did some pilots'), so there is organizational AI appetite, but no stated demand for AI in procurement. Merlin and Merlin Intake are a Zycus value-add, not a buyer requirement.

items.detail: Do-nothing / inertia is the primary threat: the group keeps procurement distributed and centralization is 'a big change for the organization', so absent a forcing event the project stays frozen rather than being lost to a rival.

critical_signals.text: The only conversation, a 19 Mar 2026 IT overview, ended with Subodh asking for a deeper business-team workshop, but that workshop was never booked and the project then froze.
There is no live rival: Ariba was replaced last year and Coupa is failing on fit for sourcing only, so the real fight is buyer inertia after a 26 May geopolitics freeze with no compelling need.
Access is stuck with two IT leaders, Subodh Kumar Dubey and Gautam Anand, both evaluative and neither advocating; procurement, finance, and business-unit pain owners have never been met.
No price, ROI, or budget owner has been established; Subodh's open question 'what is the investment' was never answered and no value case has been built.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: An early-stage P2P evaluation that has gone cold: after a single 19 Mar 2026 IT overview the buyer froze the project on 26 May for geopolitics with no compelling need, and with no champion, no mapped budget owner, and 108 days of silence the only path is a re-engagement that manufactures urgency.

win_position.text: This is a frozen, single-touch Qualified deal: one 19 Mar 2026 overview call, an explicit 26 May freeze citing geopolitics and 'no compelling need', and 108 days of buyer silence with the promised June follow-up lapsed.
No champion and no economic buyer: access is single-threaded to two evaluative IT leaders, and Subodh's own question 'what is the investment' was never answered by a finance or business budget owner.
The buyer is openly skeptical of SaaS fit after Coupa and Ariba both disappointed, so Zycus must prove configurability before it can advance rather than assuming the win.
The one genuine edge is that both prior tools failed on fit and Coupa runs only sourcing, leaving Zycus the sole P2P vendor engaged and a clean wedge if it can prove app-extend and iSaaS fit.

deal_momentum.text: Momentum is negative: the buyer-proposed April workshop was never scheduled and no meeting, task, or call has occurred since 19 Mar 2026.
The only recent CRM movement is an internal owner reassignment to Mohamad Alhakim on 29 Jun 2026, with no buyer engagement attached.

customer_commitment.text: Commitment is low: the buyer treated 19 Mar as 'a preliminary call' to 'understand how the solution is', requested no proposal, and named no decision date before freezing the project.
The buyer declined even an onsite visit ('we are in a hostile environment currently') and set no restart trigger, so there is no forward obligation on their side.

deal_risk.text: The dominant risk is do-nothing inertia plus a geopolitics freeze with no compelling event, not a competitive loss.
A parallel 12-to-18-month S/4HANA transformation could keep P2P subordinated in IT priority through 2026.

deal_momentum.evidence: No buyer activity in 108 days; proposed April workshop never scheduled; project frozen 26 May 2026.

customer_commitment.evidence: Buyer framed 19 Mar as preliminary, requested no proposal, named no decision date, and then froze the project.

deal_risk.evidence: Do-nothing freeze for geopolitics with no compelling need, no champion, and an unmapped economic buyer.

customer_preference.evidence: Buyer wanted only to 'understand how the solution is' on a preliminary call, raised repeated SaaS-fit skepticism from the Coupa/Ariba experience, requested no Zycus-specific commercial next step, and then froze the project.

business_case.evidence: Qualitative pain is clear (ineffective OpenText, no vendor portal, distributed procurement, centralization goal) but the buyer quantified no savings, cycle-time, headcount, or ROI target; 'no compelling need at present' per 26 May 2026.

evidence_coverage.salesforce_window: Activities last 120d; field history full record; contact roles; Next_Step

Economic buyer / budget owner for the P2P investment is genuinely unknown across all sources.
No procurement, finance, or business-unit stakeholder has been engaged; access is single-threaded to two IT leaders.
